nodes.title: Build a comprehensive knowledge base for software quality attributes
nodes.description:
Create a Jekyll-based static site that catalogs quality attributes, requirements, and standards from ISO 25010 and other sources, with interactive visualization of their relationships.

The arc42 quality model aims to be a practical reference for software architects working with quality requirements.

Sources:
- 747c706: 'first (pre-alpha) version with CNAME and jekyll-config'
- 1f96b0f: 'Initial commit'

nodes.title: How to organize quality content in Jekyll
nodes.description:
Jekyll offers several mechanisms for organizing content: posts, collections, pages, and data files. Quality attributes need to support tags, cross-references, and stable permalinks for graph integration.

Sources:
- c55adbb: 'fixed collection and tags for qualities, default layout now working'
- 86502f7: 'migrated to posts, as tags don't work properly in collections'

nodes.title: Use Jekyll collections for qualities
nodes.description:
Jekyll collections allow custom content types with frontmatter, but in 2022 tag functionality was broken for collections.

Sources:
- c55adbb: 'fixed collection and tags for qualities, default layout now working'

nodes.title: Use Jekyll posts for qualities
nodes.description:
Jekyll posts have built-in tag support and better integration with Jekyll's taxonomy features.

Sources:
- 86502f7: 'migrated to posts, as tags don't work properly in collections'

nodes.title: Initial content model: collections vs posts (2022)
nodes.description:
Initially tried collections, but tags didn't work properly. Switched to posts which had built-in tag support.

Sources:
- c55adbb: 'fixed collection and tags for qualities, default layout now working'
- 86502f7: 'migrated to posts, as tags don't work properly in collections'
- a163dce: 'tags working, but not looking good'

nodes.title: Migrated qualities to posts with tags
nodes.description:
Qualities were organized as Jekyll posts with tags for categorization. Each quality attribute became a post with frontmatter containing tags, related qualities, and permalinks.

Sources:
- 86502f7: 'migrated to posts, as tags don't work properly in collections'
- 7fe4437: 'page per top-level quality'
- 7638142: 'started defining terms, improved order of navigation'

nodes.title: Quality content grew to 220+ attributes using posts model
nodes.description:
The posts-based model worked well for years, allowing steady growth from initial terms to over 220 quality attributes organized alphabetically. Requirements were added as a separate category of posts.

Key milestones:
- 42 terms (2022, acfb8bf)
- ~100+ with ISO-25010 coverage (2022-2023)
- 220+ qualities + 140+ requirements (by 2025)

Sources:
- acfb8bf: 'started with maintainability, now 42 terms'
- 2806b61: 'even more terms from ISO-25010'

nodes.title: Posts model insufficient for multi-collection graph generation
nodes.description:
When graph visualization was being developed (2025), the posts-based model made it harder to distinguish qualities from requirements programmatically. Collections provide better separation of content types, which the graph data generator needs.

Sources:
- a8d514a: 'first try in making qualities a collection'
- 57551fd: 'moved requirements to category instead of blog-posts'

nodes.title: Revisit content organization: back to collections (2025)
nodes.description:
With Jekyll maturity and the need for graph data generation, the team revisited the 2022 decision to use posts. Collections now provided better content type separation needed for the graph visualization system.

Sources:
- a8d514a: 'first try in making qualities a collection'
- bbc675b: 'more migrating qualities to collections'
- d834d96: 'more transition to collections'

nodes.title: Migrated qualities and requirements to Jekyll collections
nodes.description:
Migrated all content from posts to proper Jekyll collections: _qualities/, _requirements/, and _standards/ became distinct collections with their own layouts and frontmatter defaults.

Sources:
- a8d514a: 'first try in making qualities a collection'
- bbc675b: 'more migrating qualities to collections'
- d834d96: 'more transition to collections'
- 57551fd: 'moved requirements to category instead of blog-posts'
- 042ec90: 'Fix search functionality - now works across all collections'

nodes.title: Collections model enables clean data generation and multi-type content
nodes.description:
The migration required fixing search, tag counts, and requirements counters, but provided clean separation of qualities, requirements, and standards as distinct content types. This enabled the graph data generator to process each collection independently.

Sources:
- 042ec90: 'Fix search functionality - now works across all collections'
- abf9dbf: 'Fix homepage tag counts to use correct collections'
- 0e2fea9: 'Fix requirements counter to use Jekyll collections instead of posts'

nodes.title: Visualize quality relationships interactively
nodes.description:
The quality model has complex relationships between qualities, requirements, standards, and properties/tags. An interactive graph could make these relationships explorable.

Sources:
- 4bcf3de: 'add (drafty!) interactive graph'
- 38bfc5a: 'Arc42 Quality Graphs - Patched from fork of Yadullah Duman'

nodes.title: Which graph visualization library? (2025)
nodes.description:
Multiple options considered for rendering an interactive force-directed graph of quality relationships.

Sources:
- 38bfc5a commit shows sigma.js was initially tried, then D3.js with graphology was adopted

nodes.title: Use sigma.js for graph rendering
nodes.description:
Sigma.js was initially explored as a graph rendering library. The Yadullah Duman fork started with sigma.js dependencies.

Sources:
- 38bfc5a: 'add sigma.js dependencies' (in squashed commit history)

nodes.title: Use D3.js with graphology for graph rendering
nodes.description:
D3.js force-directed layout with graphology for graph data structures. More flexible and widely supported.

Sources:
- 38bfc5a: 'display minimal graph with quality property nodes' (after sigma.js removal)

nodes.title: Implemented D3.js force-directed graph with esbuild pipeline
nodes.description:
Built the graph system with: data generation from frontmatter (data.js), D3.js rendering (GraphRenderer.js), base Graph class with HomeGraph and FullGraph variants, esbuild bundling pipeline, and JSON data files (nodes.json, edges.json).

The initial implementation by Yadullah Duman was patched and merged into the main repo.

Sources:
- 38bfc5a: 'Arc42 Quality Graphs - Patched from fork of Yadullah Duman: add script to generate data from frontmatter, display full quality graph page, add prettier and improve esbuild step, highlight connections when hovering nodes'
- a99e7b9 - 1384b91: iterative refinement of graph layout and rendering

nodes.title: Interactive graph showing qualities, requirements, and property nodes
nodes.description:
Initial graph rendered quality and requirement nodes with property (tag) nodes as connectors. Node colors distinguished types: blue for qualities, red for requirements, grey for properties. Homepage shows a compact graph, fullpage shows the complete graph.

Sources:
- 1384b91: 'Arc42 Quality Graphs - d3 #173'
- 4bcf3de: 'add (drafty!) interactive graph'

nodes.title: Graph not visible in production - data generation missing from build
nodes.description:
The graph worked in development but was invisible in production because the GitHub Actions build pipeline didn't include the data generation step.

Sources:
- 48df150: 'Fix graph not visible in production by integrating data generation into build process'

nodes.title: Integrated data generation into CI/CD build process
nodes.description:
Fixed build.js to run data generation before JavaScript bundling, ensuring graph data is available in production builds.

Sources:
- 48df150: 'Fix graph not visible in production by integrating data generation into build process'

nodes.title: How to manage graph complexity with many node types?
nodes.description:
As the graph grew to include qualities, requirements, properties, and standards, it became visually overwhelming. Users needed ways to filter what they see.

Sources:
- ec1154a: 'Graph Node Toggles - Added toggles incl. Styling - Added initial Toggling'
- 5a5f3c4: 'Graph Node Toggles - Added virtual edges for requirements only mode'

nodes.title: Added node type toggles and virtual edges
nodes.description:
Implemented toggles to show/hide different node types (qualities, requirements, properties). Added virtual edges to maintain graph connectivity when intermediate nodes are hidden.

Sources:
- ec1154a: 'Graph Node Toggles - Added toggles incl. Styling - Added initial Toggling'
- 5a5f3c4: 'Graph Node Toggles - Added virtual edges for requirements only mode'
- 98a14c0: 'Graph Node Toggles - Fixes Filtering and Displaying of Nodes in different toggle states'

nodes.title: Filterable graph with node type toggles
nodes.description:
Users can now toggle visibility of different node types. Virtual edges maintain connectivity when nodes are hidden.

Sources:
- 98a14c0: 'Graph Node Toggles - Fixes Filtering and Displaying of Nodes in different toggle states'

nodes.title: How to integrate standards into the graph?
nodes.description:
Standards (ISO 25010, GDPR, NIST, etc.) needed to be shown as nodes in the graph, connected to the qualities they cover. This required changes to both data generation and graph rendering.

Sources:
- e5db2b4: 'Complete categorized standards implementation with visual verification'
- ccd74ed: 'Revert - Complete categorized standards implementation'

nodes.title: First attempt: categorized standards implementation
nodes.description:
Initial implementation added categorized standards to the graph with visual verification.

Sources:
- e5db2b4: 'Complete categorized standards implementation with visual verification'

nodes.title: Categorized standards implementation reverted
nodes.description:
The initial categorized standards implementation was immediately reverted the next day, suggesting it didn't work correctly or introduced regressions.

Sources:
- ccd74ed: 'Revert Complete categorized standards implementation with visual verification - This reverts commit e5db2b4'

nodes.title: Iterative standards integration over 2 weeks
nodes.description:
After the revert, standards were added to the graph iteratively over many commits across two weeks (Sep 27 - Oct 7, 2025), with careful incremental integration rather than a big-bang approach.

Sources:
- 55ad39e through 41032f4: 12+ commits titled 'Graph Node Standards' across 2025-09-27 to 2025-10-07
- a41a5ad: 'Merge branch feature/graph-standards'

nodes.title: Standards integrated as gold-colored nodes in graph
nodes.description:
Standards appear as large gold (#FFC95C) nodes in the graph, connected to qualities that reference them. Standard nodes are size 45 (largest), making them visually prominent.

Sources:
- a41a5ad: merged feature/graph-standards branch

nodes.title: Added graph filtering, synonym tooltips, and center view
nodes.description:
Continued graph improvements: multi-filter support, synonym-aware tooltips, node highlighting on filter match, and a center view button for better navigation.

Sources:
- 1f5f604: 'Graph Filtering'
- 027e05e: 'feat: add graph tooltip hover for synonym display'
- 0767165: 'feat: add synonym-aware graph data generation'
- 7c577e8: 'Add Center View button and improve zooming behavior'
- cce0113: 'highlight nodes that match filter criteria'
- f9075d3: 'Simplify and restructured code, fixed standard selection reload and filtering behaviour'

nodes.title: Mature graph with filtering, tooltips, and navigation controls
nodes.description: The graph now supports: multi-criteria filtering, synonym-aware search, node type toggles, center view navigation, hover highlighting of connected nodes, and proper zoom/pan behavior.

nodes.title: Standardize quality requirements format
nodes.description:
Quality requirements were written in inconsistent formats - some as scenarios, some as bullet lists, some as prose. Need a consistent, measurable format.

Sources:
- 9c2c456: 'add scenario template'
- 058b9c2: 'propose simplified scenario template'

nodes.title: How to structure quality requirements? (2024)
nodes.description:
Initial decision to use a scenario template for structuring quality requirements.

Sources:
- 9c2c456: 'add scenario template'
- 058b9c2: 'propose simplified scenario template'

nodes.title: Full scenario template (SEI style)
nodes.description:
Traditional software quality scenario format with stimulus, source, response, measure.

Sources:
- 9c2c456: 'add scenario template'

nodes.title: Simplified scenario template
nodes.description:
A simplified version of the scenario template, more accessible for practitioners.

Sources:
- 058b9c2: 'propose simplified scenario template'

nodes.title: Converted existing requirements to simplified pattern form
nodes.description:
Batch conversion of existing requirements to the simplified pattern format.

Sources:
- 0a4d51a: 'converted all A-scenarios to pattern form'
- 7d4d315: 'converted D-scenario to pattern form'
- 4cbfe6c: 'two more requirements examples converted to new structure'

nodes.title: Requirements partially standardized but inconsistencies remained
nodes.description:
Many requirements were converted to the simplified pattern, but as more requirements were added by different contributors, inconsistencies crept back in - mixed heading levels, different section structures, varying levels of measurability.

Sources:
- 882fae4: 'Standardize subsection heading level to #### across _requirements'
- 9d5b88b: 'Fix #169: replace bold labels with headings'

nodes.title: Inconsistencies persisted despite template - need more structure
nodes.description:
Despite the simplified template, requirements remained inconsistent. A comprehensive audit revealed 106 out of 114 requirements needed conversion, with mixed naming patterns, heading levels, and varying specificity.

Sources:
- 3c398d3: 'docs: add comprehensive requirements unification plan - Create prioritized todo list for converting all 106 remaining requirements: HIGH priority (43 files), MEDIUM priority (41 files), LOW priority (22 files). Already complete: 8 files. Progress: 8/114 complete (7%)'

nodes.title: Adopt two-tier requirements system (2025)
nodes.description:
Created a two-tier system: Tier 1 for simple, self-explanatory requirements (1-3 criteria) and Tier 2 for complex requirements needing context (4+ criteria, compliance/security). This acknowledged that not all requirements need the same level of structure.

Sources:
- 3c398d3: 'docs: add comprehensive requirements unification plan'

nodes.title: Batch standardization of 100+ requirements to Tier 1/Tier 2
nodes.description:
Systematic conversion of all requirements to the two-tier format over November 2025. Each requirement was classified and reformatted with proper headings, measurable acceptance criteria, and optional context/trigger sections.

Sources:
- da9900a through many commits (2025-11-29/30): batch conversion including:
  - 'requirements: standardize low-change-failure-rate to Tier 1'
  - 'requirements: standardize withstand-ddos-attack to Tier 2'
  - 'requirements: standardize response-time-for-image-rendering to Tier 2'

nodes.title: Consistent two-tier requirements across entire collection
nodes.description:
All 140+ requirements now follow the Tier 1/Tier 2 format with measurable acceptance criteria. The REQUIREMENTS_TEMPLATE.md documents the system for future contributors.

Sources:
- c24d758: 'content: merge requirements template into quality requirements article'

nodes.title: Add industry standards as first-class content
nodes.description:
Quality attributes reference industry standards (ISO, NIST, IEEE, etc.). Making standards a proper collection with cross-references enriches the quality model.

Sources:
- a605432: 'add some standards (still DRAFT)'
- bcabbee: 'add standards page'

nodes.title: Built standards collection with 29+ standards
nodes.description:
Created _standards/ collection with entries for ISO 25010, ISO 27001, ISO 26262, IEC 61508, MISRA-C, NIST 800-53, NIST Privacy Framework, NIST AI RMF, GDPR, SOC2, WCAG 2.2, EN 301 549, IEEE 7000, ETSI EN 304 223, and more. Each standard has standard_id for cross-referencing.

Sources:
- 87e6909: 'add CIA triad information to iso-27001'
- 5023577: 'go for iso-26262'
- 5e6d874: 'initial version of IEC-61508 functional safety standard'
- 135cece: 'add misra-c standard'
- fde1f82: 'add nist 800 standard'
- 6331b4e: 'content: add NIST AI RMF 1.0 standard'
- fbe230c: 'content: add NIST Privacy Framework standard'
- d62cc94: 'content: add IEEE 7000-2021 standard on ethical system design'
- 71ab1aa: 'content: add WCAG 2.2 accessibility standard'
- C292338: 'Add ETSI EN 304 223 AI cybersecurity standard'

nodes.title: 29+ standards connected to qualities via cross-references
nodes.description:
Standards collection covers general quality models, safety standards, security frameworks, accessibility standards, and AI-specific standards. Each standard is cross-referenced from relevant quality attributes.

Sources:
- 2ba0ee4: 'added iso 25019, updated colors, added standards-todo'

nodes.title: Establish consistent visual identity across content types (2025)
nodes.description:
With four content types (qualities, requirements, standards, properties), each needed a distinct visual identity in both the graph and the site UI.

Sources:
- b66aee2: 'added proposal for new color scheme'
- 47d7e1f: 'updated color scheme'

nodes.title: Designed and implemented 4-type color scheme
nodes.description:
Established color scheme: blue (#00B8F5) for qualities, red/pink (#ffb3b3) for requirements, gold (#FFC95C) for standards, grey (#f8f9fa) for properties. Applied across graph, headers, search results, and metamodel diagrams.

Initially requirements were green, then changed to red for better distinction.

Sources:
- 47d7e1f: 'updated color scheme'
- 8eb85de: 'Change requirements color from green to red across all files'
- 10c9d7e: 'updated new colour scheme on metamodel and exported webp files'
- 4f78f31: 'Graph Colors - Adjusted colors to the new ones'

nodes.title: Unified visual identity across all content types
nodes.description:
All content types are now visually distinct with consistent colors in graph nodes, page headers, search results, and metamodel diagrams.

Sources:
- 8f4a6ff: 'documentation of colour schema'

nodes.title: Terminology mismatch: 'properties' vs 'dimensions' vs 'tags'
nodes.description:
The top-level categorization of qualities (usable, secure, efficient, etc.) was variously called 'properties', 'tags', or 'categories' in different parts of the codebase. In February 2026, a decision was made to standardize on 'dimensions' as the domain term.

Sources:
- 2b1a37a: 'renamed properties to dimensions, fixing #314'
- e326516: 'fixing several sub-issues of #312 (update domain language)'
- 952b61c: 'change metamodel to domain-language'

nodes.title: Renamed properties to dimensions across codebase
nodes.description:
Systematic renaming of 'properties' to 'dimensions' in the metamodel, UI, and documentation. Also renamed specific dimensions (e.g., 'flexible' became 'maintainable').

Sources:
- 2b1a37a: 'renamed properties to dimensions, fixing #314'
- cbb9605: '#337, initial change of dimensions flexible maintainable'
- f76ccd1: 'styling of dimension subpages, better contrast'

nodes.title: Consistent domain language using 'dimensions'
nodes.description: The metamodel and UI now consistently use 'dimensions' for the top-level quality categorization.

nodes.title: Add approaches/solutions as a new content type
nodes.description:
Beyond describing quality attributes and requirements, practitioners need guidance on HOW to achieve qualities. Approaches (like Feature Toggles, Plugin Architecture, Responsive Design) bridge the gap between quality goals and implementation.

Sources:
- 6aeafd7: 'how the approaches-properties-qualities graph might look like'
- 96022d4: 'unified some includes, added approach as new category'

nodes.title: Created approaches collection with explorer page
nodes.description:
Added approach pages for Progressive Disclosure, Responsive Design, Plugin Architecture, and Feature Toggles. Initially tried Mermaid diagrams for visualization, then replaced with verbal descriptions. Created approaches-explorer page and added approaches to the header navigation.

Sources:
- e4f11bf: 'content(approaches): add Progressive Disclosure approach'
- f986f62: 'content(approaches): add Responsive Design approach'
- 4ae4d20: 'content(approaches): add Plugin Architecture approach'
- 9f123fe: 'content(approaches): add Feature Toggles approach'
- 8f561fb: 'fix(approaches): enable Mermaid rendering for all approach pages'
- 0ac627f: 'refactor(approaches): replace mermaid diagrams with verbal descriptions'
- 1bf9827: 'add approaches-explorer, add approaches to header'

nodes.title: Approaches content type established but still growing
nodes.description:
Approaches are now a content type with a dedicated explorer page and header navigation. The content is still in early stages with 4 approaches added. Mermaid diagrams were tried and abandoned in favor of verbal descriptions.

Sources:
- 8da4d75: 'updated standards and approaches explorer page'
- ddeaaf5: 'add a planning document for approaches'

nodes.title: Site should practice what it preaches about quality
nodes.description:
As a quality model site that includes accessibility as a quality attribute and WCAG as a standard, the site itself should meet accessibility standards. Missing alt attributes and poor color contrast were found.

Sources:
- 4406a28: 'fix: add missing alt attributes to images (WCAG compliance)'
- 7bcb6de: 'updated colour schema to be better wcag compliant'

nodes.title: Added Playwright tests for WCAG compliance
nodes.description:
Introduced Playwright-based automated testing for WCAG compliance. Added tests, fixed missing alt attributes, improved color contrast, and added WCAG score display to the navbar.

Sources:
- b344c8b: 'add playwright tests'
- 5354c5d: 'added wcag test'
- 4406a28: 'fix: add missing alt attributes to images (WCAG compliance)'
- 7bcb6de: 'updated colour schema to be better wcag compliant'
- ebd5583: 'ui improvements, add wcag score to navbar'
- 5fd9953: 'feat: make wcag-report full width with back-to-home CTA button'

nodes.title: Automated WCAG testing with compliance score in navbar
nodes.description: The site now has Playwright-based WCAG tests, improved color contrast, proper alt attributes, and a WCAG compliance score displayed in the navigation bar.

edges.rationale: Need to organize quality attributes in Jekyll
Posts model creates friction for graph data generation
Standards content ready for graph integration
After standardizing requirements, add solution approaches
Color scheme audit revealed WCAG issues
Collections model enabled clean graph data generation
